dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a diabetic problem.
Hello, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
Chief complaint is hypoglycemia with altered mental status. According to the wife, she heard strange noises 15 minutes ago and found the patient on the couch groaning and snoring.
He is initially responsive to painful stimuli with a GCS of 8. He is not answering questions at this time.
Sir, can I quickly assess your skin and pulse?
His skin is pale, cool, and clammy with a strong radial pulse present. No major bleeding or edema noted.
Let's check your blood glucose level. Initial BG is 34-this is very low, consistent with hypoglycemia.
Vital signs: pulse 76, respirations 16, blood pressure 149/71, oxygen saturation 95%. EKG is showing sinus rhythm with no dysrhythmias or ectopy.
Given the patient's history of type 1 diabetes and hypothyroidism (on insulin and levothyroxine), we need to initiate protocol 3-8. I will place the patient on a 4-lead ECG.
ECG is on and shows sinus rhythm-no evidence of dysrhythmias or ectopy.
Partner, let's start IV access for administration of dextrose as per protocol.
IV started in the patient's left forearm with one successful attempt.
Administering 100 mL of D10 after completing a two-provider medication cross check.
The patient received 100 mL of D10 per protocol after verification from both providers.
Blood glucose has improved to 94 five minutes after the D10 administration. His GCS has improved to 14.
Pulse has increased to 92, but respirations remain 16 and blood pressure is now 0/0-likely transient.
Ma'am, he seems quite alert after eating. His mental status is improving. Would you mind telling me what the full date is today?
October 29th, 2020.
Good recall. Now, what is your first name? And mine?
I'm Joseph. And you're Alex.
Good job. He's A&Ox4 now-alert, oriented to time and person. He even knows my name.
Pulse is now 82, respirations 16, blood pressure is 134/82, oxygen saturation remains at 99%. EKG is still showing sinus rhythm.
Blood glucose increases to 258 mg/dL. His mental status is now fully oriented-A&Ox4 with no confusion.
No pain reported at this time. Pain rating remains at 0/10.
Ma'am, he is medically stable, not in distress, and is now alert. He may choose to refuse transport provided he understands the risks. Are you sure you do not want to go to the ER?
I understand the risks, but I choose not to be transported. I'll stay home and call my PCP tomorrow.
Ma'am, we highly recommend that you stay with him and monitor closely, and if you have any concerns, call 911 immediately.
The patient has decided not to be transported and is medically stable with A&Ox4 and BGs returning to normal.
He and his wife have been informed of the risks of refusing transport. A signed refusal has been obtained.
Protocol 3-8 was appropriately followed for hypoglycemia. Patient is now at baseline and does not require EMS transport.
Documenting refusal of care and transport and initiating return to service.
